title: Multi-Criteria Filter
description:
Build a **product filter** that combines multiple filter criteria (search text, category, price range) managed with `useReducer`.

### Requirements

- `filterReducer` handles: `SET_SEARCH`, `SET_CATEGORY`, `SET_PRICE_RANGE`, `RESET_FILTERS`
- State shape: `{ search: string, category: string, priceRange: [number, number] }`
- Initial state: `{ search: '', category: 'all', priceRange: [0, 1000] }`
- Derive `filteredProducts` by applying **all** active filters simultaneously
- Search is case-insensitive and matches product name
- Category filter: `'all'` shows everything, otherwise exact match
- Price range filter: `product.price >= min && product.price <= max`
- Display the count of matching products

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Search input | `search-input` |
| Category select | `category-select` |
| Min price input | `min-price` |
| Max price input | `max-price` |
| Reset button | `reset-filters` |
| Result count | `result-count` |
| Product card | `product-{id}` |
constraints:
- Must use useReducer for filter state
- Filtering logic must handle all criteria simultaneously
- Do not mutate state in the reducer
- Search should be debounce-friendly (though debounce is not required)
hints: Apply all filters in sequence using .filter() chaining or a single .filter() with compound conditions.
For the category filter, check `category === 'all' || product.category === category`.
Use useMemo to derive filteredProducts so it only recalculates when state or products change.
